title: Tirumanthamkunnu Temple
tagline: Tirumanthamkunnu Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Thirumandhamkunnu Temple is a historically significant Hindu temple in Angadipuram, which was the capital of Valluvanad Rajavamsham, in Malappuram district, Kerala state, South Ind
overview.description: Thirumandhamkunnu Temple is a historically significant Hindu temple in Angadipuram, which was the capital of Valluvanad Rajavamsham, in Malappuram district, Kerala state, South India. The temple deity, Thirumandhamkunnil amma, was the paradevatha (official goddess) of the kings of Valluvanad, the local feudal kings ruled the area in the Middle Ages. The Nair warriors (called Chavers, literally "martyrs") of Valluvanad king set out from this temple to Thirunavaya, to participate in the famous Mamankam festival. topPlaces.name: Angadipuram
topPlaces.description: Angadippuram is a village and a census town in Perinthalmanna taluk and major suburb of Perinthalmanna town, in the Malappuram District of Kerala. It was the capital of the powerful medieval kingdom of Valluvanad.

topPlaces.name: Angadipuram Laterite
topPlaces.description: Angadippuram Laterite is a notified National Geo-heritage Monument in Angadippuram town in Malappuram district in the southern Indian state of Kerala, India. The special significance of Angadippuram to laterites is that it was here that Dr.

topPlaces.name: Valambur
topPlaces.description: Valambur is a town in Malappuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Perinthalmanna
topPlaces.description: Perinthalmanna is a town and a municipality in Malappuram district, Kerala, India. It serves as the administrative centre for the Perinthalmanna taluk, as well as a revenue division, a community development block, and a police sub-division bearing the same name.

topPlaces.name: Manathmangalam
topPlaces.description: Manathmangalam, also known as Manathumangalam is an important commercial area and an electoral ward of Perinthalmanna Municipality, Malappuram district in Kerala State of India. It's also a neighbourhood of Perinthalmanna.

topPlaces.name: Pariapuram
topPlaces.description: Pariapuram is a dhesam (village sub-division) situated near Angadipuram village of the Malappuram District of Kerala in the country of India. The nearest town is Perintalmanna.

topPlaces.name: Aripra
topPlaces.description: Aripra (Malayalam: അരിപ്ര) is a village in India located between the villages of Mankada and Tirurkkad, in Malappuram district, Kerala. National Highway No.966 passes through the center of this village.

topPlaces.name: Pattikkad, Perinthalmanna
topPlaces.description: Pattikkad is a town near Perinthalmanna, in Malappuram district of Kerala, in southwestern India. It is situated at about 5 km from Perinthalmanna, in the Valancheri- Perinthalmanna-Nilambur road.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tirumanthamkunnu Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tirumanthamkunnu Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Tirumanthamkunnu Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (32 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (84 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tirumanthamkunnu Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tirumanthamkunnu Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tirumanthamkunnu Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Tirumanthamkunnu Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tirumanthamkunnu Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Angadipuram, Angadipuram Laterite, Valambur, Perinthalmanna, Manathmangalam.
